title: trihaloragis hexandra
intro: Trihaloragis hexandra, also known as the greater toothbrush tree or Ailanthus excelsa, is a plant species that belongs to the family Simaroubaceae. The medicinal properties of Trihaloragis hexandra include its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which are attributed to its alkaloid and glycoside content. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Trihaloragis hexandra is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its herbal preparations can also be used topically as a cream or ointment to treat skin conditions.
